legs.summary: The adherence evidence says the spec solves the reported issue. The graded rows were rendered as noise because the spec already covers the dscli logs command, worker selection, default tail count, locality/read-only behavior, follow mode, and aggregation scope.

The adherence evidence says the plan is underspecified against the spec. It identifies a blocking gap for the explicit local-host-only/read-only operational safety requirement, plus an advisory omission for warning when a worker is stopped.

The adherence evidence says the code implements the plan with an overplan verdict. Its graded rows are noise because the command class, arguments, run path, PID helper, command registration, primary-path test, and docs are all traced as present.

The mm-compliance evidence says the implementation diverges, with 1 blocking divergence. The divergence is that the operator-facing command hardcodes a log path and filename that may not match the configured LogManager output used by normal worker deployments.

The consistency evidence says documentation updates are inadequate. The design spec and plan are treated as noise, but the operator-facing dscli reference is missing the new dscli logs command and its -w/-n options.

The consistency evidence says tests are inadequate. The blocking test gap is that cli/tests/test_logs.py verifies tail invocation and success but does not assert that returned log lines are written to stdout.
The security evidence reports PASS. It lists no locked violations.
